sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to place in a row, to set in order; Arab. سار Med. Waw, reihen, ordnen. Hence שֹׂורָה series, order, row; which see.

(2) to be leader, commander, prince, i.q. שׂוּר No. I. 2, and שָׂרַר; from setting in order, arranging soldiers. Arab. سَرُوَ to be noble, liberal; سَِرىُّ a prince, a noble.

(3) to fight (prop. to wage war) with any one; followed by עִם Gen 32:29 followed by אֵת Hos 12:4 (Arab. شرا Conj. III. id.). Fut. is taken from שׂוּר No. I.

Derivatives, שֹׂורָה, מִשְׂרָה and pr. names שָׂרַי, שְׂרָיָה, יִשְׂרָאֵל.

שרה

f. of the noun שַׂר-

(1) a princess, a noble lady, Jud. 5:29 Est 1:18 Isa 49:23 specially of the wives of a king of noble birth, 1Ki 11:3 who differed from the concubines; comp. Son 6:8. Metaph. Lam 1:1, שָׂרָתִי בַּמְּדִינֹות “princess of the provinces.”

(2) pr.n. Sarah; see שָׂרַי .
